eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Nicolás Maduro at 61.7% to serve as Venezuela's head of state by end-2026, driven by the Venezuelan Supreme Court's January 2026 ruling deeming his U.S. capture a temporary "kidnapping" that preserves his constitutional presidency, alongside uncertain UN recognition of successor claims. Delcy Rodríguez, sworn in as interim president shortly after, holds 23.5% amid her recent military commander replacements in March and U.S. sanction relief enabling IMF funds and trade resumption, yet persistent repression and sluggish democratic reforms raise doubts about her longevity. Opposition leaders like María Corina Machado linger below 6%, hampered by institutional control and rumored coalition talks, with Maduro's U.S. trial outcome a key looming catalyst.

markets.question: Will Partido Socialista Obrero Español de Andalucía (PSOE-A) win 27-29 seats in the 2026 Andalusia Regional Election?
markets.description:
Elections for the autonomous community of Andalusia’s unicameral parliament are scheduled for May 17, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the number of seats won by Partido Socialista Obrero Español de Andalucía (PSOE-A) in the Parliament of Andalusia as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by Partido Socialista Obrero Español de Andalucía (PSOE-A) in this election.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as published by the Spanish Junta Electoral Central (JEC) (https://www.juntaelectoralcentral.es/cs/jec/inicio).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices PSOE-A at 30-32 seats (41%) as the leading outcome for the May 17 Andalusian regional election to the 109-seat Parliament, reflecting the upper range of recent April polls projecting 26-34 seats (averaging 27-28 from CIS, Sigma Dos, GAD3, and others). This positioning stems from PSOE-A's modest recovery from 2025 lows under leader María Jesús Montero, bolstered by fallout from the PP-led government's October 2025 breast cancer screening scandal—exposing 2,000+ delayed diagnoses, record manipulation, and Health Minister resignation—which has eroded the incumbent's "good manager" image without collapsing their projected 53-58 seats. Vox's 13-20 seat gains and left fragmentation (Por Andalucía, Adelante Andalucía) constrain PSOE upside, while 24-26 seats (29.5%) captures downside risk in this former socialist stronghold amid national PSOE headwinds. Final campaign pushes and turnout could tip ranges before resolution.

markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Justin Fields sits at 25.5% following his March trade from the Jets as primary insurance, with Andy Reid's April endorsement calling him a "legitimate starting NFL QB" ready for early reps if needed. markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 1160-1199 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

events.title: Middlesbrough FC vs. Southampton FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming EFL Championship game, scheduled for Saturday, May 9, 2026 between Middlesbrough FC and Southampton FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Middlesbrough enter the EFL Championship playoff semi-final first leg at Riverside Stadium as trader consensus slight favorites at 41.5% implied probability, buoyed by home advantage and a commanding 4-0 victory over Southampton in their January home league clash. Both sides clinched top-six finishes amid final-day drama, with Boro drawing Wrexham to secure fourth place ahead of Saints in fifth, setting up this promotion decider. Defensive injury concerns linger for Middlesbrough (Darragh Lenihan, Alfie Jones out long-term) and Southampton (Toby Harwood-Bellis ankle, Jan Bednarek knee doubts), potentially tightening defenses in a high-stakes tie. Recent form shows both competitive in the table race, but Boro's strong home record edges the closely contested market, where Saints (29.5%) and draw (28.5%) reflect upset potential.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Miami be 79°F or below on May 6?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Miami Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 6 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Miami Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/fl/miami/KMIA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a tight race between 88-89°F (40%) and 86-87°F (35%) for Miami's highest temperature on May 6, reflecting National Weather Service forecasts and model ensembles like GFS and ECMWF projecting peaks around 86-88°F amid building high pressure. Recent decreases in shower coverage over South Florida since early May have spurred a warming trend, with sea surface temperatures near 82°F fueling daytime heating, though sea breeze fronts and cumulus cloud development introduce uncertainty—potentially capping highs at 86-87°F if onshore flow strengthens, or pushing to 88-89°F under clearer skies. Early May climatology averages 84-86°F, but current ridge dominance favors the upper end; watch morning soundings and 12Z model updates for shifts before resolution at Miami International Airport.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 United States presidential election will be held on Tuesday, November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

Upcoming economic data and midterm outcomes could shift these probabilities in the closely watched battleground states.

markets.question: Uber # of trips above 4.6B in Q1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Uber’s total number of trips for the first fiscal quarter of 2026, as reported in its official company earnings materials, is above the listed amount. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The specified metric will be considered as reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Subsequent revisions will not be considered.

If the specified company’s official earnings materials for the specified quarter are released and the specified metric is not included, this market will resolve to “No”. 

If the specified company does not release quarterly earnings materials for the specified quarter by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified metric is reported as a range rather than a specific number, the midpoint of the range will be used for resolution of this market. 

The resolution source for this market is Uber’s official company earnings materials, including press releases, investor presentations, and regulatory filings. If the specified metric is not reported in these materials, recordings or transcripts of the company’s earnings webcast may also be used.

Note: This market will resolve based on the most numerically precise version of the specified metric reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Only the specified metric will be considered; alternate versions that differ in definition or scope from the specified metric will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for Uber's Q1 2026 trips hinges on tomorrow's earnings release on May 6, where the official figure—tracking platform engagement and gross bookings—will resolve markets. Management's $52-53.5 billion gross bookings guidance implies ~20% constant-currency expansion versus Q1 2025's 3.0 billion trips, bolstered by resilient mobility demand, delivery gains, and international scaling. Consensus revenue forecasts $13.27 billion, though seasonal Q1 softness and fuel cost pressures temper expectations for peak quarterly volume.

markets.question: T20 World Cup, East Asia-Pacific Qualifier, Regional Final: Philippines vs Indonesia - Toss Match Double Philippines Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Philippines and Indonesia scheduled for 2026-05-07 in T20 World Cup, East Asia-Pacific Qualifier, Regional Final.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Philippines will be considered correct if  Philippines is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Indonesia will be considered correct if Indonesia is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices the toss at even 50% implied probabilities for Philippines, Indonesia, and draw in this Group 2 opener of the ICC Men's T20 World Cup East Asia-Pacific Qualifier 2026 at neutral Sano International Cricket Ground, Japan, on May 8, underscoring the coin flip's randomness with no captain or venue bias evident. Philippines enter with strong recent form, winning four of their last five T20Is against Indonesia—including a dominant 78-run victory on December 11, 2025—and boasting a W L W W W W streak, while Indonesia slumped to a loss against Malaysia on May 1 amid an L L W L L L run. Absent pitch reports or weather updates, teams lack clear preference for batting or bowling first, keeping the pre-match ritual tightly contested.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will US withdraw from NATO by...?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: The Pentagon's May 2 announcement of withdrawing 5,000 US troops from Germany over the next six to 12 months—leaving over 30,000 troops—has intensified transatlantic tensions, driven by European allies' refusal to back US actions in the Iran conflict. President Trump has repeatedly signaled frustration with NATO burden-sharing, threatening withdrawal in April interviews and discussions with Secretary General Mark Rutte, echoing his first-term criticisms. However, Section 1250A of the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act requires congressional approval for any US exit, facing bipartisan opposition and historical precedent against unilateral presidential action on treaties. NATO officials are assessing the troop repositioning, with no formal denunciation process underway amid ongoing alliance diplomacy.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/in/lucknow/VILK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent thundery showers and cloud cover have driven unseasonably low temperatures in Lucknow, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) recording a May 4 maximum of 29°C—10.4°C below normal—amid 14.8 mm rainfall, suppressing daytime heating. IMD forecasts partly cloudy skies with further rain/thundershowers likely through May 7 across Uttar Pradesh, including Lucknow, where May 5 highs are expected around 31°C before a gradual 4-6°C rise, potentially reaching 36-38°C per extended range outlooks for central plains. Trader consensus favors ≤35°C at 36% implied probability, reflecting persistent atmospheric instability capping peaks below typical May norms of 39-42°C, though haze and clearing could enable rebound; monitor IMD's May 5 evening bulletin for updated model consensus.

markets.question: Will HOTU qualify to IEM Cologne Major 2026?

Europe secured 17 slots, seeding Vitality, Natus Vincere, PARIVISION, Aurora, Falcons, and MOUZ directly into Stage 3's Bo3 Swiss starting June 11, while Stage 1 opens June 2 featuring tier-2 squads like HEROIC, B8, and Team Liquid in Bo1/Bo3 Swiss. markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Europe at 71.5% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, driven by UEFA's 16 slots featuring seven of the top 10 FIFA-ranked nations including leaders Spain, France, and England, bolstered by March 2026 playoff triumphs for Bosnia and Herzegovina, Sweden, Turkey, and Czechia over rivals like Italy and Ukraine. South America's 20.5% reflects CONMEBOL's powerhouse sextet—Argentina, Brazil, Uruguay, Colombia, Ecuador, Paraguay—but tempered by Brazil's Rodrygo suffering a torn ACL ruling him out until late 2026, a blow highlighted in recent injury reports. Africa (3.6%) and Asia (3.4%) gained via inter-confederation playoffs with DR Congo and Iraq, yet lack historical pedigree; North America's hosts (2.6%) and Oceania (0.3%) face steep barriers amid the expanded 48-team field starting June 11.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

The June 23 closed primary pits Jeffries against underfunded challenger Vance Bostic after progressive Councilman Chi Ossé withdrew last December, while the Republican primary features Lewis Mizrahi and Richard Simmons amid no recent polling. Forecasters rate it Safe or Solid Democratic. Late-breaking scandal, health event, or extraordinary national Republican wave could challenge this outlook before November 3 resolution.

markets.question: Will David Lisnard be on the ballot for the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held in April 2027. Prior to the election, the French Constitutional Council is expected to publish the official list of candidates to be included on the ballot for the first round of this election.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed individual is included on the official candidate list for the first round of the 2027 French presidential election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no such candidate list is published prior to the election, this market will resolve based on which candidates actually appear on the ballot when the election takes place. If no list is published and the specified election does not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official candidate list published by the French Constitutional Council; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With President Emmanuel Macron term-limited after confirming no post-2027 political role, the 2027 French presidential election ballot remains wide open amid fragmentation, with over 10 declared candidates including Édouard Philippe (Horizons), Bruno Retailleau (Les Républicains, party-nominated April 19), Jean-Luc Mélenchon (La France Insoumise, announced May 3), Jérôme Guedj (Socialist Party), and minor figures like Nathalie Arthaud (Lutte Ouvrière). National Rally eyes Jordan Bardella if Marine Le Pen's ineligibility appeal fails July 7. A left-wing primary is set for October 11, while signatures (500 parrainages from 30+ departments) begin collecting early 2027 ahead of the April first round. Post-March municipal elections, polls favor Bardella in hypotheticals, boosting expectations for major-party qualifiers.

markets.question: Will Algeria vs. Austria end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for June 27, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Algeria vs. Austria
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Saturday, June 27, 2026 between Algeria and Austria.

eventMetadata.context_description: Austria holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 43% implied probability for victory over Algeria (37.5%) in this pivotal 2026 FIFA World Cup Group J finale at neutral Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City, with a draw at 29.5% underscoring the tight contest between evenly ranked sides (Austria 24th, Algeria 28th FIFA). Algeria's mounting injury crisis—goalkeepers Anthony Mandrea (shoulder surgery) and Melvin Mastil (groin hernia) sidelined, plus Yacine Titraoui (hamstring) and Amine Gouiri (muscle)—has eroded squad depth just weeks from kickoff, tempering their strong African qualifying campaign. Austria, buoyed by recent friendlies wins over Ghana (5-1) and South Korea (1-0) in late March, leverages superior recent form and defensive solidity despite their own concerns like Maximilian Wöber's muscular issues, keeping probabilities bunched in this high-stakes neutral-venue showdown absent recent head-to-head history.

events.title: Another US debt downgrade before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States' long-term sovereign credit letter rating is downgraded by any of the three major credit rating agencies (S&P, Moody's, Fitch) at any point by December 31, 2026 11:59pm ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Standard & Poor's, Moody's, or Fitch, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 74.5% implied probability against another major US sovereign credit rating downgrade before 2027, reflecting Fitch Ratings' April 30, 2026 affirmation of its AA+ stable outlook despite projecting US debt-to-GDP at 122% by year-end—up from 116.6% in 2025—amid widening fiscal deficits averaging $1.9 trillion annually per CBO estimates. This stability follows Moody's Aa1 downgrade in May 2025 and prior S&P/Fitch actions, with agencies citing chronic governance gridlock but no acute triggers like debt ceiling default risks, as the $41.1 trillion limit provides headroom above current $39 trillion levels. Key catalysts include FY2027 budget talks and potential debt limit hikes, where bipartisan resolutions have historically averted crises, underscoring the resilience of US Treasuries backed by global reserve demand.

markets.question: Will Liverpool finish in the top 4 of the EPL 2025–26 standings?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs finish in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club officially finishes in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more clubs are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official English Premier League tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With three matchweeks remaining in the 2025/26 Premier League season, Arsenal (76 points from 35 games) and Manchester City (71 from 34, with a game in hand) have secured top-four finishes and Champions League qualification, bolstered by City's dominant goal difference (+37) despite a recent 3-3 draw at Everton that kept Arsenal's title lead at five points. Manchester United cling to third on 64 points (+15 GD), but face pressure from Liverpool and Aston Villa, both on 58 points after 35 games and capable of hitting 67 maximum; the pair's identical records intensify the battle for fourth amid head-to-head form, home advantages, and looming fixtures against mid-table sides. No major injuries reported among contenders, though fatigue from Chelsea-Man City FA Cup final on May 16 could factor for City. Trader consensus reflects United's buffer versus the live chase below.

markets.question: Will Khamenei post 20-24 posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Khamenei (@khamenei_ir), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a low volume of posts from the official @khamenei_ir X account—archival statements from the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei—at under 5 (56.5%) or 5-9 (41.5%) during May 1-8, driven by its prolonged silence since the April 13 post amid the leadership transition following his February 28 martyrdom in US-Israeli strikes. Recent US intelligence reports detailing severe injuries to successor Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, including limited official access, have fueled expectations of subdued activity, contrasting the new @MKhamenei_ir account's May 1 cluster of nine posts marking Workers' and Teachers' Days. Earlier April markets resolved around 10-14 posts, but three weeks without updates signal trader caution on resumption.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% after its April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting—its third consecutive pause—despite March CPI inflation accelerating to 3.3% year-over-year from 2.4% in February, while unemployment held near 4.3%. Chair Powell's press conference underscored a data-dependent stance amid balanced risks, aligning with the March dot plot's median projection of 3.4% by end-2026. Polymarket traders, wagering over $1 million, reflect this consensus via skin-in-the-game pricing, with focus shifting to April CPI on May 12, June 16-17 FOMC deliberations, and labor data for cues on potential easing before 2027.

markets.question: Will T1 make a roster change before July?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player joins or leaves T1's active League of Legends roster (defined as the active lineup listed on the T1 Liquipedia page: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/T1) by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

As of market creation the active roster consists of the following players: Doran, Oner, Faker, Peyz, and Keria. Name changes or aliases referring to the same player do not qualify as roster changes.

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different active roster.

Coach changes and temporary stand-ins for a single match will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be Liquipedia; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Les Wexner charged by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Leslie Wexner between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Les Wexner charged by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Leslie Wexner between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no criminal charges against Les Wexner by June 30, reflecting the absence of any DOJ indictment despite heightened scrutiny from recently released Epstein files. In February 2026, a House Oversight Committee deposition saw Wexner, Epstein's longtime financial patron, deny knowledge of the sex offender's crimes, claiming he was duped; lawmakers questioned his credibility but took no further prosecutorial steps. A civil lawsuit filed by Epstein survivors in early April alleges Wexner enabled the abuse through funding and property, yet distinguishes from criminal proceedings. With no active federal investigation or scheduled hearings announced, and Wexner uncharged since Epstein's 2019 death, traders price significant barriers to near-term prosecution.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no in-person meeting between President Trump and President Putin by June 30, reflecting the absence of any announced bilateral summit amid ongoing phone diplomacy, including their April 29 call—their 12th since early 2025—where they discussed Ukraine truce possibilities around Russia's May 9 Victory Day without scheduling a face-to-face. Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov confirmed no plans for a meeting during Putin's recent China visit, while Trump's reported invitation for Putin to the December G20 summit in Miami falls after the deadline. Low odds on sites like China or Turkey stem from unconfirmed speculation, with Ukraine ceasefire negotiations and Iran tensions handled remotely so far; a sudden diplomatic breakthrough or escalation could prompt an urgent neutral-site summit to challenge this positioning.

markets.question: Exact Score: FC Bayern München 4 - 4 Paris Saint-Germain FC?
markets.description: In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game between FC Bayern München and Paris Saint-Germain FC, scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the FC Bayern München vs. Paris Saint-Germain FC match originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Paris Saint-Germain's thrilling 5-4 first-leg semifinal victory over Bayern Munich in the UEFA Champions League, trader consensus reflects extreme uncertainty for the second leg at Allianz Arena, with top exact-score outcomes tightly bunched around 49.5% implied probabilities. Bayern trail by one goal on aggregate and must attack relentlessly at home, leveraging their five straight prior Champions League wins over PSG, but face defensive vulnerabilities exposed by PSG's clinical finishing from Khvicha Kvaratskhelia and others. Bayern's injury woes—Serge Gnabry (thigh, season-ending), Raphaël Guerreiro (hamstring), and Lennart Karl (out)—weaken depth, while PSG remain largely healthy despite Quentin Ndjantou's hamstring absence. Both sides' recent high-scoring form (PSG eight wins in nine, Bayern potent in knockouts) signals another open, goal-filled affair with no clear scoreline favorite.

markets.question: Rui Hachimura: Rebounds O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 5 at 12:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rui Hachimura records more than 3.5 total rebounds during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Rui Hachimura records 3.5 rebounds or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

events.title: Lakers vs. Thunder
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 5 at 12:00AM ET:
If the Lakers win, the market will resolve to "Lakers".
If the Thunder win, the market will resolve to "Thunder".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder host the Los Angeles Lakers in Game 1 of the Western Conference semifinals at Paycom Center, where OKC's regular-season dominance—outscoring LA by an average 29.3 points per matchup—fuels trader consensus alongside home-court advantage and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-level form through the first round. Pivotal injury updates from the past 24 hours confirm Thunder wing Jalen Williams out with a left hamstring strain and Lakers star Luka Doncic sidelined by left hamstring recovery, thrusting pressure onto 41-year-old LeBron James and LA's veteran core amid back-to-back road challenges ahead. Thunder's depth and playoff momentum contrast LA's upset path, with recent first-round advances heightening stakes for series momentum.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol remains in custody following his February 2026 conviction and life sentence for insurrection tied to the 2024 martial law declaration, with an appeals court on April 29 increasing his term to seven years for obstruction of arrest. These severe penalties, upheld amid multiple detention warrant extensions, reflect judicial consensus on charges from the political crisis that led to his impeachment and ouster by the Constitutional Court in April 2025. Absent successful higher appeals or extraordinary pardons—unlikely given the timeline to December 31, 2026—traders price a 92.5% implied probability of "No," emphasizing entrenched legal barriers over release scenarios.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Paris be 11°C on May 6?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 6 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fr/bonneuil-en-france/LFPB.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 15°C (41.5% implied probability) and 14°C (40.5%) as Paris's highest temperature on May 6, driven by the latest Météo-France guidance forecasting a 14°C peak under persistent overcast skies and light northerly winds (5-10 km/h), contrasted by ensemble model spreads from ECMWF and GFS hinting at possible 15°C if brief diurnal cloud breaks allow extra insolation. Yesterday's thundery showers entrenched a cool air mass from a recent low-pressure perturbation, capping temperatures below May's 18°C climatological norm despite moderate solar angle. Key differentiators include cloud opacity variations and light rain risks; evening forecast updates from NOAA and European models could shift odds before resolution via official Paris station observations.

markets.question: Beijing Guoan FC vs. Dalian Yingbo FC: O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Chinese Super League game between Beijing Guoan FC and Dalian Yingbo FC, scheduled for May 6 at 7:35 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Beijing Guoan FC and Dalian Yingbo FC combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on csl-china.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Beijing Guoan FC vs. Dalian Yingbo FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Chinese Super League game, scheduled for May 6 at 7:35 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Dalian Yingbo's impressive early-season form, including a 2-0-2 record placing them 6th in the Chinese Super League table, contrasts with Beijing Guoan's mid-table position around 12th following a 2-0 loss to Liaoning Tieren on April 4 amid defensive injuries to goalkeeper Jiaqi Han, center-back Uros Spajic, and right-back Yupeng He, plus recent concerns for Guilherme Ramos. Beijing holds a strong head-to-head edge with two wins and one draw over Dalian Yingbo, including a 4-2 home victory in September 2025 at Workers' Stadium, where the May 6 clash returns. Traders weigh Guoan's home advantage and historical dominance against Yingbo's momentum from recent away results like their upset bid versus Shandong Taishan.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Morgan Stanley as the frontrunner at 50% implied probability to serve as lead bank for SpaceX's anticipated mega-IPO, code-named Project Apex, targeting a $1.75 trillion valuation and June 2026 listing, driven by its longstanding advisory role with Elon Musk—including leading Tesla's 2010 IPO—and recent reports confirming it among active bookrunners alongside Goldman Sachs (17.5%) and Bank of America (16.2%). Early April Reuters and CNBC disclosures revealed a 21-bank syndicate with these firms in senior roles after SpaceX's April 6 banker meeting and confidential SEC filing, elevating their odds amid competitive bake-offs; lower probabilities for Citigroup (1.8%) and others reflect supporting positions. Traders eye late-May prospectus release and June roadshow as key catalysts amid SpaceX's Starlink-driven growth and reusable rocket milestones.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Joe Wilson's strong position drives 83% trader consensus for a Republican Party win in SC-02, anchored by the district's R+7 partisan lean—Trump's 14-point 2024 margin—and Wilson's $294,000 cash on hand dwarfing GOP primary challengers Sam Gibbons and Hamp Redmond. Democratic Party odds at 17.5% align with Wilson's consistent 55-60% general election victories against underfunded Democratic primary contenders Zyon Khalifa, Roger Pruitt, David Robinson II, and Daniel Shrief. Candidate-specific markets—A, B, and Other—trade near 50% on low volume amid pre-primary uncertainty ahead of the June 9 contests. No notable developments have emerged in the past 30 days, with Cook Political Report rating the seat Solid Republican.

“Released” means the full episode is published and available to the general public on an official UpOnly platform (e.g. YouTube, Spotify, Apple Podcasts, or the show’s official feed).

Previews, clips, teasers, or promotional material do not count toward resolution.

The resolution source will be the official UpOnly podcast channels and/or a consensus of credible public reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's latest observations and 9-day forecast, updated on May 5, project a daily temperature range of 20–23°C amid a moderating northeast monsoon bringing slightly cooler conditions and occasional showers to the region. Trader consensus at 99.9% implied probability for a 19°C low reflects confirmed early-morning readings at key stations hovering around this mark—such as 19°C at Tsuen Wan Ho Koon on similar recent days—with the Observatory's minimum typically set by dawn under cloudy skies and moderate easterly winds force 4–5. Historical May mean minima around 22–23°C provide context for this modest dip from an upper-air trough, though diurnal warming limits further cooling. Scenarios challenging this include unexpected clear skies accelerating overnight radiational cooling or a stronger monsoon surge, but model consensus shows low likelihood; watch HKO's end-of-day summary for final resolution.

markets.question: UFC Fight Night: Bernardo Sopaj vs. Timmy Cuamba (Bantamweight, Main Card)

It will resolve to "Timmy Cuamba" if Timmy Cuamba is officially declared the winner.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: In this closely contested bantamweight bout on the UFC Vegas 117 main card, Timmy Cuamba (10-3, UFC 2-2) carries momentum from back-to-back wins, including a flying knee KO of Roberto Romero in April 2025 and unanimous decision over ChangHo Lee in November 2025, leveraging his 5'9" frame, 71" reach, and striker style (2.77 SLpM). Bernardo Sopaj (12-3, UFC 1-1), a high-volume orthodox striker (4.24 SLpM, 56% accuracy) with solid wrestling (2.53 TD avg.), returns after a 16-month layoff following his January 2025 unanimous decision rebound over Ricky Turcios, post-KO loss to Vinicius Oliveira. No recent injuries or cancellations reported ahead of May 16 at the APEX; stylistic clash favors Sopaj's pressure against Cuamba's distance management.

markets.question: Will Marta Kostyuk win the 2026 Women’s French Open?

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Women’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Women’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Aryna Sabalenka at 31.5% implied probability to win the 2026 Women's French Open on clay at Roland Garros, driven by her strong recovery in the Mutua Madrid Open—where she overcame break deficits in early matches after skipping Stuttgart due to a minor injury—contrasting Iga Świątek's struggles, including a third-round retirement from illness against Ann Li in Madrid and a 2-2 clay record this swing. Elena Rybakina's 13.6% reflects her consistent form, including Stuttgart quarterfinals and leading the WTA Race standings ahead of Sabalenka, while Coco Gauff's 8.5% and Mirra Andreeva's 7.0% highlight their steady clay results amid Kostyuk's surprise Madrid title. With the Italian Open looming, surface history favors Świątek long-term, but recent health and momentum shifts explain the positioning.

markets.question: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, North and South Korea engage in direct talks, defined as an official meeting or communication between government representatives of both sides, conducted without a third-party relaying messages, by June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

The talks may be in-person, by phone, or virtual, and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by credible media. 

Routine military deconfliction, backchannel exchanges, or talks conducted entirely through another country or organization will not count. 

The resolutions source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, North and South Korea engage in direct talks, defined as an official meeting or communication between government representatives of both sides, conducted without a third-party relaying messages, by June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

The talks may be in-person, by phone, or virtual, and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by credible media. 

Routine military deconfliction, backchannel exchanges, or talks conducted entirely through another country or organization will not count. 

The resolutions source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no direct talks between North and South Korea by June 30, reflecting Pyongyang's entrenched rejection of inter-Korean dialogue amid its "two hostile states" policy toward Seoul. Despite South Korean President Lee Jae-myung's repeated calls for renewed negotiations since March and a brief thaw in early April—sparked by Seoul's apology for a civilian rally and reciprocal positive rhetoric—North Korea clarified on April 8 it seeks no clashes but explicitly rules out talks. No summits, working-level meetings, or official channels have been announced, with Kim Jong Un prioritizing military ties to Russia and signaling openness to separate U.S. diplomacy. Absent major de-escalation signals or scheduled diplomacy on the Korean Peninsula, traders see significant barriers to direct engagement within the timeline.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 3 match between TLN Pirates and Esprit Shōnen in the LFL Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 6 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if TLN Pirates and Esprit Shōnen play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: TLN Pirates vs Esprit Shōnen (BO3) - LFL Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 3 match between TLN Pirates and Esprit Shōnen in the LFL Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 6 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "TLN Pirates" if TLN Pirates win the match against Esprit Shōnen.

This market will resolve to "Esprit Shōnen" if Esprit Shōnen win the match against TLN Pirates.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: TLN Pirates, fresh off a dominant 7-2 regular season finish as the No. 2 seed in LFL 2026 Spring, face No. 7 Esprit Shōnen in the upper bracket quarterfinals BO3 on May 6 under fearless draft rules. Pirates capped the round-robin with a 1-0 win over Shōnen on May 1, extending their head-to-head edge amid just two losses all split to teams like Vitality.Bee and ZYB Esport. Shōnen's 3-6 record reflects inconsistency despite a stable roster featuring xKenzuke mid and Mxe support, while Pirates' core of Spooder top, Stefan jungle, and Toffe mid drives trader consensus toward their seeding advantage in this double-elimination playoff path to EMEA Masters qualification. No roster changes or substitutes reported post-regular season.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

events.description:

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 22, 2026, parliamentary elections, which produced a hung parliament in the 90-seat National Assembly, no single party secured the 46 seats needed for a majority government, with Prime Minister Robert Golob's Freedom Movement (GS) taking 29 seats and rival Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) claiming 28. Golob's attempts at a broad coalition excluding SDS collapsed by April 20, prompting him to concede and position GS in opposition; President Nataša Pirc Musar declined to nominate a prime minister-designate on April 25, urging lawmakers to propose a candidate capable of building a majority. A centre-right bloc of SDS, New Slovenia (NSi) with 9 seats, Democrats (DEM), and Resni.ca—collectively holding 48 seats—has advanced coalition talks, including proposals to trim ministries as a step toward nominating a prime minister for a confidence vote, amid ongoing negotiations that could tip the balance.

markets.question: Will Mistral have the #2 AI model at the end of June 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will Raluca Turcan be the next Prime Minister of Romania?
markets.description:

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed by the President of Romania and receive a vote of confidence from Parliament, resulting in the official formation of a new government. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister who does not receive a parliamentary vote of confidence will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Romania; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Parliament's no-confidence vote on May 5 toppled Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan's pro-EU coalition government, following the Social Democratic Party's (PSD) April withdrawal amid budget disputes and financial pressures, plunging Romania into fresh political crisis. President Nicușor Dan must now consult parliamentary groups to nominate a new premier for a confidence vote, with trader consensus favoring Interior Minister Cătălin Predoiu at 42% implied probability due to his prior 2025 interim premiership, technocratic profile, and National Liberal Party (PNL) ties as a stabilizing pro-EU figure. PSD leader Sorin Grindeanu trails at 14.5%, boosted by his party's plurality and demands for swift government formation, while far-right AUR's George Simion at 6.5% reflects nationalist undercurrents from the 2024 election annulment. Coalition negotiations and the looming confidence vote will drive near-term shifts.

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal hold a strong position heading into their UEFA Champions League semi-final second leg against Atletico Madrid at the Emirates on May 5, following a dominant 4-0 league-phase win over the Spanish side in October. Atletico's momentum has stalled after a heartbreaking Copa del Rey final penalty-shootout loss to Real Sociedad on April 18, where key forwards Ademola Lookman and Alexander Sorloth sustained injuries, casting doubt on their fitness for the first leg at the Metropolitano on April 29 and exposing defensive vulnerabilities. Leading the Premier League table, Arsenal contend with Martin Odegaard's ongoing knee issue and Bukayo Saka's ankle doubt ahead of their April 25 Newcastle clash, while Atletico sit fourth in La Liga amid a form dip—traders weigh home-soil resilience against Arsenal's attacking firepower and head-to-head edge.

markets.question: Will Adam Schiff be arrested before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual is arrested by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying arrest/detention includes:
* Being taken into physical custody by law enforcement authorities (including federal, state, local, military, or international law enforcement)
* Voluntarily surrendering to law enforcement authorities in response to an arrest warrant
* Being formally booked or processed following detention
* Being handcuffed by a state authority and taken to a police station
* Surrendering at a police station or courthouse with their attorney
* Being placed under house arrest or electronic monitoring

The following scenarios will NOT qualify as an arrest/detention
* An arrest warrant being issued but not executed
* Being briefly detained but not arrested (e.g. such as in the South Korean judicial system when an individual is awaiting a judge’s decision on whether to grant a detention warrant)
* Being questioned or interviewed without arrest
* Being named in an indictment without arrest

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the arresting government and law enforcement, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump publicly insult someone on May 16, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump makes any public statement in which he insults, mocks, or attacks any non-fictional individual personally or professionally in a clearly negative manner on the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

This includes calling the individual weak, stupid, disloyal, a failure, using an insulting nickname, using other derogatory language, or using the negative form of a positive trait in a derogatory personal way (e.g., “He/She isn’t smart”). Negative forms used in reference to the individual's professional actions, policies, or decisions (e.g., “He/She isn’t being smart about this policy”) will not count. Policy disagreements stated without disparaging language will not count.

A direct reference will qualify even if the individual is not named, so long as it is reasonably clear from context that they are the subject.

Any written, verbal, or recorded public statement by Trump qualifies.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

markets.question: Spread: Manchester City FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for March 21 at 11:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Manchester City FC" if Manchester City FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Crystal Palace FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Sitting second in the table with 61 points from 30 matches, nine behind leaders Arsenal, City view this game in hand as pivotal for their title push amid a tight top-four race including Manchester United and Aston Villa. Palace, mid-table battlers, face defensive injury woes with Daniel Munoz, Cheick Doucoure, and Jean Mateta sidelined long-term, while City manage knocks to Nico O'Reilly and Josko Gvardiol alongside Mateo Kovacic's Achilles absence. Recent City form shows rotation depth, but home advantage and superior goal difference favor them upon rescheduling.

markets.question: Will Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) hit (HIGH) $215 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.COIN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.COIN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.
